mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with falling federal funds rates indicate accommodative monetary policy successfully stimulating consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% monthly while FEDFUNDS declines, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during periods of elevated market volatility, or directional accuracy drops below 45% in stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 900,000 units, the housing sector contraction triggers negative wealth effects and reduced construction spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where HOUST drops below 900,000, or the threshold signal loses predictive significance in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside stagnant or rising federal funds rates indicate restrictive real policy conditions that suppress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising CPIAUCSL and non-declining FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative returns, or the interaction effect reverses sign during supply-driven inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms sustained economic expansion, reinforcing earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during periods of financial market dislocation, or directional accuracy falls below 50% in rolling 3-year tests.
